Chapter 5 • Argumentasi dan Pendapat

Your opinion can move people.

Di chapter ini, siswa tidak hanya membaca teori. Siswa diajak masuk ke masalah nyata di Sapuka, memilih pendapat, membangun alasan, memperbaiki kalimat, berdebat sopan, menulis exposition text, lalu membuat produk digital.

Explore Sapuka issuesBuild an argument
12model texts
8local issues
6grammar labs
5creation missions

Learning Journey

Siswa belajar dari masalah nyata, lalu naik level sampai mampu membuat argumen dan karya digital.

Student Path
1

Notice

Melihat isu di sekitar: HP, pantai, rob, air, internet, rumput laut.

2

Think

Menentukan pendapat: agree, disagree, atau balanced opinion.

3

Build

Menyusun reason, example, connector, dan conclusion.

4

Speak & Write

Melatih pendapat dalam dialog, speech, dan exposition text.

5

Create

Membuat poster, carousel, voice-over, podcast, atau campaign video.

Sapuka Issue Cards

Pilih satu isu yang dekat dengan kehidupan siswa, lalu jadikan bahan opinion, argument, speech, atau digital creation.

Local Context
Sapuka Issue

HP untuk belajar

Sebagian siswa memakai HP untuk kamus, video, dan materi. Namun, HP juga bisa mengganggu fokus jika dipakai untuk game atau media sosial.

Sapuka Issue

Internet Starlink

Internet membantu siswa membuka materi digital, tetapi tidak semua siswa selalu punya perangkat atau kuota. Materi offline tetap penting.

Sapuka Issue

Pantai dan plastik

Pantai adalah bagian dari kehidupan masyarakat pulau. Sampah plastik dapat mengotori pasir, laut, dan membahayakan biota laut.

Sapuka Issue

Rumput laut dan sekolah

Sebagian siswa membantu keluarga mengikat atau memanen rumput laut. Mereka perlu belajar mengatur waktu agar tetap fokus sekolah.

Sapuka Issue

Banjir rob

Saat pasang besar, beberapa jalan rendah tergenang. Siswa bisa harus berjalan ke sekolah sambil melepas sepatu.

Sapuka Issue

Air bersih dan Sumur Seribu

Saat kemarau, air bisa sulit dan asin. Masyarakat mencari cara untuk mendapatkan air tawar atau agak tawar.

Sapuka Issue

Ombak putih

Pada musim gelombang besar, kapal dan kegiatan melaut bisa tertunda. Keselamatan menjadi sangat penting.

Sapuka Issue

English outside classroom

Siswa beginner sering malu berbicara. English Club dan latihan ringan bisa membuat speaking terasa lebih aman.

Learning Objectives

Setelah mempelajari chapter ini, siswa mampu:

Fase E
  1. Mengenali exposition text sebagai teks untuk menyampaikan pendapat dan meyakinkan pembaca melalui alasan yang logis.
  2. Membedakan opinion, fact, reason, argument, evidence/example, reiteration, dan recommendation dalam teks sederhana.
  3. Mengidentifikasi struktur exposition text: thesis, arguments, dan reiteration/recommendation pada teks bertema kehidupan siswa Sapuka.
  4. Menggunakan opinion expressions, connectors, modal verbs, simple present tense, dan persuasive vocabulary untuk membuat argumen.
  5. Menyusun argumen bertahap dari isu nyata: HP untuk belajar, English Club, pantai bersih, internet, air bersih, rumput laut, rob, dan disiplin sekolah.
  6. Menulis short exposition text yang memiliki thesis jelas, minimal dua arguments, contoh nyata, dan penutup yang tepat.
  7. Menyampaikan pendapat secara lisan dengan sopan melalui agree/disagree, giving reasons, dan one-minute opinion speech.
  8. Membuat digital creation berupa poster, carousel, voice-over, mini podcast, atau campaign video berbasis exposition text.

Visual Learning Gallery

Gambar digunakan sebagai pintu masuk diskusi, bukan sekadar hiasan.

Picture Prompts

Understanding Exposition Text

Exposition text membantu kita menyampaikan pendapat dengan alasan, bukan sekadar suka atau tidak suka.

Core Concept

What is exposition text?

Exposition text adalah teks yang menyampaikan pendapat penulis tentang suatu isu dan mendukung pendapat itu dengan alasan yang logis.

Tujuannya adalah meyakinkan pembaca bahwa pendapat tersebut masuk akal.

From opinion to argument

Opinion: Students should join English Club.

Reason: because it helps them practice speaking.

Argument: Students should join English Club because it helps them practice speaking and build confidence.

PartMeaningExample
OpinionPendapat pribadiI think students should read more books.
ReasonAlasan mengapa pendapat itu masuk akalbecause reading improves vocabulary.
Evidence / ExampleContoh nyata yang memperkuat alasanFor example, students can read short stories and English comics.
ArgumentOpinion + reason + exampleStudents should read because reading improves vocabulary. For example, they can read short stories.

Text Structure

Setiap exposition text yang baik biasanya memiliki tiga bagian besar.

Structure Map
1

Thesis

Memperkenalkan isu dan pendapat utama penulis.

I believe that students should use mobile phones for learning.
2

Arguments

Berisi alasan, contoh, dan penjelasan yang mendukung thesis.

First, mobile phones help students find information quickly.
3

Reiteration / Recommendation

Menegaskan kembali pendapat atau memberi saran tindakan.

Therefore, students should use mobile phones wisely.

Deep Grammar Lab

Grammar dipelajari sebagai alat untuk membuat pendapat lebih jelas, sopan, dan kuat.

Language Tools
1. Simple Present Tense

Dipakai untuk fakta umum, kebiasaan, dan pendapat.

Students need clean water. Reading improves vocabulary. Plastic waste pollutes the sea.
2. Opinion Expressions

I think..., I believe..., In my opinion..., From my point of view..., I strongly agree that..., I do not agree that...

In my opinion, students should use phones only for learning activities.
3. Giving Reasons

because + sentence. because of + noun phrase.

Correct: Students feel tired because they help their parents. Correct: Students feel tired because of long activities. Incorrect: because of they help their parents.
4. Connectors for Arguments

First, Second, Third, Moreover, In addition, However, Therefore, For example, As a result.

However, students must use mobile phones responsibly.
5. Modal Verbs

Use base verb after modal.

Students should keep the beach clean. Schools must support students. We can learn from digital materials.
6. Persuasive Language

important, necessary, useful, dangerous, harmful, beneficial, responsible, wisely, reliable, inclusive.

Offline materials are important because they make digital learning more reliable.

Opinion-to-Argument Builder

Siswa dapat mencoba membangun exposition paragraph sendiri. Ketik ide, lalu lihat preview-nya.

Interactive Lab

Your exposition paragraph

Vocabulary Bank

Gunakan search dan kategori untuk menemukan kata yang diperlukan saat menulis argumen.

Bilingual
WordMeaningContextExample
opinionpendapatUsed when we say what we think.In my opinion, students should read every day.
argumentargumen/alasan pendukungUsed to support an opinion.My first argument is that reading improves vocabulary.
reasonalasanExplains why an opinion makes sense.The reason is that students need more practice.
evidencebuktiInformation that supports an argument.Real examples can become evidence.
examplecontohMakes the reason clearer.For example, students can use a dictionary app.
reiterationpenegasan ulangRestates the main opinion at the end.Therefore, reading is important for students.
recommendationsaran/anjuranSuggests what readers should do.Therefore, students should reduce plastic waste.
WordMeaningContextExample
assignmenttugasSchool work from teacher.Students should submit assignments on time.
confidencekepercayaan diriFeeling brave to do something.English Club builds confidence.
fluencykelancaranAbility to speak smoothly.Speaking practice improves fluency.
dictionary appaplikasi kamusUsed to check meaning and pronunciation.A dictionary app helps students learn new words.
offline materialmateri luringUseful when internet is weak.Offline materials help students in remote areas.
school clubklub sekolahA place to practice skills outside class.School clubs help students grow.
WordMeaningContextExample
island areawilayah kepulauanA place surrounded by sea and far from mainland.Students in island areas need strong learning support.
seaweedrumput lautA local product and family activity.Some students help their parents with seaweed after school.
tidal floodbanjir robFlood caused by high tide.Tidal flood can make students walk through water.
beach abrasionabrasi pantaiDamage caused by waves.Beach abrasion is a serious problem in coastal areas.
clean waterair bersihWater that is safe to use.Students need clean water at school.
sea safetykeselamatan lautStaying safe during sea travel or bad weather.Sea safety is important during big waves.
WordMeaningContextExample
becausekarenaGives a reason.Students should read because reading improves vocabulary.
howevernamunShows contrast.Phones are useful. However, students must use them wisely.
moreoverselain ituAdds another strong point.Moreover, English gives students more opportunities.
thereforeoleh karena ituShows conclusion.Therefore, we should keep the beach clean.
importantpentingMakes an argument stronger.Clean water is important for health.
responsiblebertanggung jawabUsed for wise behavior.Students should be responsible phone users.

12 Model Texts

Teks model dibuat variatif: isu sekolah, pulau, keluarga, internet, lingkungan, dan speaking confidence.

Reading Studio
Model Text 1Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 1Hortatory ExpositionHP untuk belajar

Students Should Use Mobile Phones Wisely for Learning

I believe that students should use mobile phones wisely for learning. Mobile phones can be useful tools when students use them in the right way.

First, mobile phones help students find information quickly. When students do not understand an English word, they can use a dictionary app and check the pronunciation.

Second, mobile phones help students learn outside the classroom. For example, students can read offline materials, watch short learning videos, or practice vocabulary after school.

However, students must use mobile phones responsibly. They should not play games, open social media, or disturb friends during lessons.

Therefore, schools should allow students to use mobile phones for learning, but students must use them wisely and follow the teacher’s rules.

Structure & language notes
  • Opinion expression: I believe that...
  • Connector: First, Second, However, Therefore
  • Modal verbs: should, must
  • Local connection: HP can support dictionary and offline learning.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 2Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 2Analytical ExpositionSEAClub / English Club

English Club Is Important for Island Students

In my opinion, English Club is important for island students. It gives students more chances to practice English in a safe and friendly place.

First, English Club helps students practice speaking. In class, some students feel shy. In English Club, they can speak with friends through games, short dialogues, and simple conversations.

Second, English Club builds confidence. Students learn that making mistakes is normal. They become braver because their friends support them.

Moreover, English Club can make English feel closer to daily life. Students can talk about the beach, boats, seaweed, school events, and their own island.

Therefore, English Club is a useful activity because it helps students improve speaking skill, confidence, and friendship.

Structure & language notes
  • Purpose: to convince readers that English Club matters.
  • Persuasive words: important, useful, confidence
  • Local connection: English can be practiced through Sapuka daily topics.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 3Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 3Hortatory ExpositionPantai bersih

We Should Keep Our Beach Clean

I strongly agree that we should keep our beach clean. For people who live on an island, the beach is not only a beautiful place. It is also part of daily life.

First, a clean beach protects the environment. Plastic waste can pollute the sand and the sea. It can also harm fish, turtles, and other sea animals.

Second, a clean beach is good for people’s health. Dirty beaches can smell bad and attract flies. Children may also get sick if they play near rubbish.

Third, a clean beach makes our island more beautiful. Local people will feel proud, and visitors will respect the island more.

Therefore, students and community members should throw rubbish in the bin, reduce plastic waste, and clean the beach together.

Structure & language notes
  • Strong opinion: I strongly agree that...
  • Recommendation: clean the beach together
  • Local connection: beach is part of island life.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 4Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 4Analytical ExpositionAkses internet

Students in Island Areas Need Better Internet Access

From my point of view, students in island areas need better internet access. Internet is not only for entertainment. It can also support learning.

First, good internet helps students get more learning materials. Students can read articles, watch educational videos, and find examples for school assignments.

Second, better internet access helps teachers share materials. In remote areas, students cannot always buy many books. Digital materials can help them learn more.

Third, internet access gives students more opportunities. They can join online competitions, learn new skills, and communicate with students from other places.

Therefore, better internet access is important for students in island areas because it supports learning and opens more opportunities.

Structure & language notes
  • Opinion expression: From my point of view
  • Local connection: remote island school
  • Balanced idea: internet supports learning but materials should stay offline-friendly.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 5Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 5Analytical ExpositionKebiasaan membaca

Reading Books Can Improve Students’ Future

I think reading books can improve students’ future. Reading is a simple activity, but it has many benefits.

First, reading improves vocabulary. When students read stories, articles, or school books, they find new words. This helps them understand English better.

Second, reading increases knowledge. Books can teach students about science, culture, history, technology, and many other topics.

Third, reading helps students think better. When students read, they learn to understand ideas, compare information, and make conclusions.

Therefore, reading books is important because it improves vocabulary, knowledge, and thinking skills.

Structure & language notes
  • Simple present tense: reading improves, reading increases
  • Reiteration: reading is important
  • Skill focus: vocabulary, knowledge, thinking.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 6Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 6Hortatory ExpositionRumput laut, keluarga, dan sekolah

Students Should Help Their Parents but Still Focus on School

I believe that students should help their parents, but they must still focus on school. Helping parents is good, but education is also important for the future.

First, helping parents teaches responsibility. Many students help their families after school, such as helping with seaweed, cleaning the house, or taking care of younger siblings.

Second, students also need enough time to study. If students are too tired, they may not finish their assignments. They may also feel sleepy in class.

Third, students need balance. They can help their parents, but they should also make time for homework, reading, and rest.

Therefore, students should help their parents responsibly and manage their time well so they can still focus on school.

Structure & language notes
  • Balanced argument: helping parents is good, but school is important.
  • Modal verbs: should, must
  • Local connection: seaweed work and family responsibilities.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 7Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 7Hortatory ExpositionLingkungan sekolah dan pulau

Schools Should Teach Students How to Protect the Environment

In my opinion, schools should teach students how to protect the environment. Students need to understand that their actions can affect nature.

First, environmental education helps students understand real problems. For example, students in island areas may see plastic waste, beach abrasion, or dirty water around them.

Second, students can learn simple actions to protect the environment. They can reduce plastic, plant trees, save water, and keep the school clean.

Third, schools can build good habits. If students learn to care for the environment at school, they may also do it at home and in their community.

Therefore, schools should teach environmental protection through lessons, projects, and real actions.

Structure & language notes
  • Recommendation: schools should teach through action.
  • Examples: reduce plastic, save water, plant trees.
  • Local issue: abrasion, dirty water, plastic waste.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 8Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 8Analytical ExpositionSpeaking confidence

Teenagers Should Be Brave to Speak English

I think teenagers should be brave to speak English. Many students understand some English words, but they are afraid to speak.

First, speaking practice improves fluency. Students cannot speak English well if they only memorize words. They need to use the language in real communication.

Second, making mistakes is part of learning. Students should not feel embarrassed when they pronounce a word incorrectly. They can learn from correction.

Third, English speaking skill can help students in the future. It can help them join competitions, continue their study, or communicate with people from other places.

Therefore, teenagers should be brave to speak English because practice builds fluency, confidence, and future opportunities.

Structure & language notes
  • Core message: mistakes are part of learning.
  • Persuasive words: brave, fluency, confidence, opportunities.
  • Speaking mission link: one-minute opinion speech.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 9Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 9Hortatory ExpositionAir bersih dan musim kemarau

Students Should Save Clean Water at School

I believe that students should save clean water at school. In island areas, clean water can become difficult to get during the dry season.

First, water is important for health. Students need water to drink, wash hands, and keep the school clean.

Second, wasting water can make the problem worse. When many students use water carelessly, the school may not have enough water for important needs.

Third, saving water teaches responsibility. Students can close the tap, use enough water, and remind friends politely.

Therefore, students should save clean water every day because small habits can help the whole school.

Structure & language notes
  • Local issue: clean water during dry season.
  • Recommendation: close the tap and use enough water.
  • Vocabulary: save, health, responsibility.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 10Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 10Hortatory ExpositionBanjir rob dan kehadiran sekolah

Schools Should Support Students During Tidal Flood Days

In my opinion, schools should support students during tidal flood days. In low areas, high tide can make the road to school wet and difficult.

First, students may need more time to arrive at school. Some students have to walk through water and take off their shoes.

Second, students still need to learn even when the situation is not easy. Teachers can give clear instructions, short tasks, or flexible time when needed.

Third, support from school can make students feel safe and respected. Students should not feel embarrassed when they arrive with wet shoes or uniforms.

Therefore, schools should prepare simple support during tidal flood days so students can keep learning safely.

Structure & language notes
  • Local issue: tidal flood/rob.
  • Balanced language: support, flexible time, safety.
  • Recommendation ending.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 11Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 11Analytical ExpositionOmbak putih dan keselamatan

Students Should Learn Sea Safety During Big Wave Seasons

I think students in island areas should learn sea safety during big wave seasons. The sea is close to daily life, so students need to understand how to stay safe.

First, sea safety knowledge helps students make better decisions. They can understand when the weather is dangerous and when they should avoid risky activities near the sea.

Second, many families depend on boats and fishing. When students understand safety rules, they can also remind family members and younger children.

Third, sea safety lessons connect school knowledge with real life. Students can learn vocabulary about weather, waves, wind, and safety instructions in English.

Therefore, sea safety is important because it protects students and connects English learning with island life.

Structure & language notes
  • Local issue: big waves/ombak putih.
  • Cross-curricular: English + safety + local life.
  • Reiteration ending.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.
Model Text 12Build a clear argument.Use the unique visual set above as shared stimulus. This section focuses on thesis, reasons, examples, and conclusion without repeating images.
Model Text 12Analytical ExpositionMateri offline-first

Offline Materials Are Still Important for Remote Schools

From my point of view, offline materials are still important for remote schools. Internet access is useful, but it is not always stable for every student.

First, offline materials help students continue learning when the signal is weak. They can read lessons, answer exercises, and review vocabulary without waiting for internet connection.

Second, offline materials are fairer for students who do not always have devices or data. They can still study from printed modules or saved HTML lessons.

Third, offline materials help teachers prepare learning in difficult conditions. Teachers can share one file that works on many devices.

Therefore, offline materials are important because they make digital learning more reliable and inclusive for remote schools.

Structure & language notes
  • Digital learning nuance: online and offline both matter.
  • Persuasive words: reliable, inclusive, fairer.
  • Good for Sapuka/offline-first context.
Comprehension questions
  1. What is the writer’s opinion?
  2. Mention one argument from the text.
  3. What example or local situation supports the argument?
  4. Is the ending a reiteration or recommendation? Explain briefly.

Text Detective: Annotated Model

Lihat bagaimana thesis, arguments, connectors, dan modal verbs bekerja di dalam teks.

Annotation

I strongly agree that we should keep our beach clean. For people who live on an island, the beach is not only a beautiful place. It is also part of daily life.

First, a clean beach protects the environment. Plastic waste can pollute the sand and the sea.

Second, a clean beach is good for people’s health. Dirty beaches can smell bad and attract flies.

Therefore, everyone should throw rubbish in the bin, reduce plastic waste, and clean the beach together.

Legend

  • Blue = thesis / opinion
  • Green = argument / reason
  • Yellow = connector
  • Pink = modal verb

Dialogue Practice

Latihan speaking dibuat dekat dengan isu siswa, bukan dialog textbook yang terlalu umum.

Speaking

Agreeing politely — English Club

Alya: What do you think about joining English Club?
Rani: I think it is a good idea because it helps us practice speaking.
Alya: I agree with you. We can learn English in a fun way.
Rani: Yes. We can also become more confident.

Disagreeing politely — Phones during class

Bima: I think students should be free to use phones during class.
Dika: I see your point, but I’m not sure I agree.
Bima: Why not? Phones are useful.
Dika: Yes, phones are useful for learning. However, some students may use them for games or social media.

Giving reasons — Clean beach

Sari: What is your opinion about plastic waste on the beach?
Nina: I think it is a serious problem.
Sari: Why?
Nina: Because plastic waste can pollute the sea and harm sea animals.
Sari: I agree. That is why we should clean the beach together.

Balanced opinion — Helping parents

Fajar: Should students help their parents after school?
Mira: I believe they should, but they also need study time.
Fajar: What is your reason?
Mira: Helping parents teaches responsibility. However, students must manage their time well.

Sentence Clinic

Kesalahan umum siswa Indonesia diperbaiki dengan penjelasan sederhana.

Common Mistakes

Agree is a verb

Wrong: I am agree.
Correct: I agree.

Jangan memakai am sebelum agree.

Opinion expression

Less natural: According to me...
Better: In my opinion...

Untuk pendapat pribadi, gunakan In my opinion.

Subject-verb agreement

Wrong: Students is important.
Correct: Students are important.

Students plural, jadi gunakan are.

Missing verb

Wrong: Because mobile phone useful.
Correct: Because mobile phones are useful.

Kalimat bahasa Inggris perlu verb.

Modal verb

Wrong: Students should studies.
Correct: Students should study.

Setelah should, gunakan verb dasar.

Because vs because of

Wrong: because of it rains
Correct: because it rains / because of the rain

Because + sentence. Because of + noun phrase.

Interactive Quiz

Quiz singkat dengan feedback agar siswa bisa mencoba ulang.

Practice

1. Opinion or fact?

“I think students should keep the beach clean.”



2. Best connector

Students should read books ___ reading improves vocabulary.




3. Polite disagreement

Choose the most polite response.



4. Structure detective

“Therefore, students should save clean water every day.”



Writing Mission

Siswa naik level dari kalimat pendek ke exposition paragraph.

Writing
Level 1

Write 3 opinions

I think..., I believe..., In my opinion...

Level 2

Add reasons

First,... because... Second,... because...

Level 3

Add local examples

For example, students can use dictionary apps, clean the beach, save water, or join English Club.

Level 4

Write a short text

Title: Thesis: I believe that... Argument 1: First,... because... Argument 2: Second,... because... Example: For example,... Ending: Therefore,...

Speaking Mission

Siswa berbicara satu menit tentang isu nyata di sekolah atau pulau.

1-Minute Speech

Topic choices

  • Students should practice English every week.
  • We should keep our island clean.
  • Students should use phones wisely.
  • Schools should support students during tidal flood days.
  • Students should save clean water.
  • Offline materials are important for remote schools.

Speaking frame

Good morning, friends. Today, I want to share my opinion about... I think... First,... Second,... For example,... That is why I believe... Thank you.

Digital Creation Studio

Produk akhir membuat siswa merasa hasil belajarnya nyata, bisa dipajang, dipresentasikan, atau dibagikan.

Create

Digital Poster

Poster berisi title, opinion, two reasons, picture, dan call to action.

Clean Beach, Healthy Island!

Carousel Slides

4 slide: issue, opinion, reasons, recommendation. Cocok dibuat di Canva/PowerPoint/HP.

Voice-over Opinion

Rekam suara 45–60 detik membaca opinion speech lalu gabungkan dengan gambar.

Mini Podcast

Dua siswa berdialog: one agrees, one disagrees politely, lalu mencari kesimpulan.

Mini Project & Reflection

Proyek akhir: My Opinion for a Better School / Island.

Final Task

Final product options

  • Short exposition paragraph
  • One-minute speech
  • Digital poster
  • Carousel slide
  • Mini podcast / dialogue video

Reflection checklist

CriteriaExcellentGoodNeeds Improvement
Clear opinionVery clear and relevantClear enoughUnclear
Logical reasonsTwo or more strong reasons with examplesAt least one clear reasonWeak or missing reasons
Language featuresUses connectors, modal verbs, and simple present correctlyUses some featuresMany errors block meaning
